clues: A common fruit used in pies.
It keeps the doctor away.
This fruit fell on Newton's head according to legend.
A large body of salty water.
Covers about 70% of Earth's surface.
Whales and sharks call this home.
A staple food made from flour and water.
It comes out of an oven golden and warm.
Sandwiches are impossible without this.
A musical instrument with black and white keys.
Beethoven composed masterpieces on this.
It has 88 keys and makes beautiful harmonies.
It floats in the sky and brings rain.
Made of tiny water droplets high in the atmosphere.
Where lightning is born.
A hard solid mineral found in nature.
You can skip it across water.
Used to build ancient castles.
It gives off heat and light.
Moths are famously attracted to this.
The thing that candles produce.
It flows from mountains to the sea.
Fish swim upstream through this.
The Nile is the world's longest example.
A tall narrow structure that rises above everything.
Rapunzel was locked in one.
The Eiffel structure in Paris is a famous one.
Artists use this to paint.
Your teeth need this every morning.
It has bristles and a handle.
Small fruits that grow in clusters on a vine.
They can be fermented into wine.
Purple or green, sweet or sour.
Athletes wear this around their necks after winning.
It can be gold, silver, or bronze.
A symbol of achievement.
It makes things sweet.
Bees make honey which contains this.
Too much of it is bad for your teeth.
Footwear that covers your ankle and sometimes your calf.
Perfect for muddy or snowy weather.
Cowboys are famous for wearing these.
Furniture designed for one person to sit on.
It has four legs and a back support.
Every office and classroom has many of these.
What does Earth belong to in the night-sky family?
It orbits a star and has gravity strong enough to be round.
Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn are examples.
It spans across rivers and valleys.
It connects two places that water separates.
The Golden Gate in San Francisco is a famous example.
It always shows you the same face.
Snow White's stepmother spoke to this daily.
A reversed reflection is what you get.
It burns slowly and lights a room in the dark.
Birthday cakes have these blown out for wishes.
Made of wax with a wick in the center.
A dense tropical forest full of wildlife.
Tarzan swung through the trees of this.
Mowgli from The Jungle Book lived here.
A large fortified medieval structure with turrets.
Kings and queens lived here to stay safe from enemies.
You can visit many in Europe.
A vast dry land where rain rarely falls.
Camels are perfectly adapted for this terrain.
The Sahara is the world's largest hot one.
What keeps a ship from drifting away?
It is thrown into the sea and holds a vessel in place.
Often tattooed on sailors' arms.
Men often keep this in their back pocket.
It stores credit cards, cash, and IDs.
You panic when you can't find it.
It spins silk webs and has eight legs.
Peter Parker was bitten by one.
Charlotte was a famous literary one.
An old-fashioned portable light source.
In many Asian festivals, they are released into the sky.
Aladdin's magical object was technically one.
A navigation tool that always points north.
Pirates and explorers relied on this to find their way.
It uses Earth's magnetic field.
The loud sound that follows a lightning strike.
It is caused by rapid air expansion.
Thor, the Norse god, was associated with this.
A shiny cup awarded to the winner of a contest.
Sports teams compete fiercely to lift this.
Your name gets engraved on it.
It follows you wherever you go in sunlight.
During a solar eclipse, Earth walks through one.
It disappears in the dark.
When one celestial body blocks another's light.
The moon can cause a solar version of this.
Ancient people feared this rare sky event.
A complex maze that's nearly impossible to escape.
The Minotaur lived in one in Greek mythology.
It symbolizes confusion and endless searching.
Communication that barely disturbs the air.
Secrets travel this way to avoid being overheard.
The opposite of a shout.
An apparition or ghostly presence.
The Opera has a famous masked one.
It exists but cannot be touched.
A toxic substance injected by some creatures.
Snakes and spiders use this as a weapon.
It can paralyze or kill its target.
Something with a hidden or mysterious meaning.
Ancient inscriptions are often described this way.
Crossword puzzles of this type are the hardest to solve.
A glowing piece of coal or wood from a dying fire.
It persists long after the flames go out.
Blow on it gently and fire might return.
A person or place believed to give divine prophecy.
Ancient Greeks consulted the one at Delphi.
It knows the answer before the question is asked.
An optical illusion caused by heat in the desert.
Thirsty travellers see this and think they've found water.
It looks real but vanishes as you approach.
A bottomless or immeasurably deep space.
Nietzsche warned that if you stare into it, it stares back.
It represents the deepest unknown.
A hard crystalline mineral found in rocks.
Watches use this to keep precise time.
It comes in many colors, including rose and smoky varieties.
A secret method of writing using coded letters.
Spies use this to pass messages safely.
Breaking one is the job of a cryptographer.
A short double-edged blade used for stabbing.
Julius Caesar was assassinated with multiple of these.
Smaller than a sword, deadlier up close.
A brilliant bright shade of red.
The Letter by Hawthorne features this color in its title.
It is deeper than crimson and brighter than burgundy.
An object surviving from the past with historical significance.
Museums preserve these from ancient civilizations.
Indiana Jones spent his career hunting these.
